title: Workorder event producers and preferences
objective: Connect workorder domain events to notifications while keeping recipient selection and privacy rules server-side.
non_goals: Do not bypass visible_workorders_queryset or policy helpers.
Do not notify users about source objects they cannot open.
Do not include full workorder descriptions in system notifications.
Do not introduce workflow rule changes unless separately approved.
requirements: Workorder create, transition, assignment and comment events must map to notification event types.
Recipients must be derived through workorder visibility and role semantics.
Authors should not receive noisy notifications about their own actions unless preferences require it.
Notification preferences must allow disabling low-priority categories.
Events must be safe and idempotent where service retries are possible.
acceptance_checks: New assigned workorder notifies the assignee.
A user outside visibility scope does not receive the notification.
Transition to resolved can notify author or responsible role according to preference.
Comment notification excludes sensitive comment body from system notification text.
Preference disabling a category stops new deliveries for that category.
